name: Entera Bio Ltd.
business: Entera Bio is a clinical-stage company focused on developing first-in-class oral tablet formats of peptides or protein replacement therapies for underserved chronic medical conditions. Its most advanced product candidate, EB613 (oral teriparatide), is being developed as the first oral osteoanabolic tablet treatment for osteoporosis. The company also has programs for hypoparathyroidism (EB612), obesity and metabolic disorders (oral GLP-1/Glucagon), and short bowel syndrome (oral GLP-2).
sellsTo: patients with obesity and metabolic disorders

exposures.sentence: Entera is a clinical stage company focused on developing first-in-class oral tablet formats of peptides or protein replacement therapies.

catalysts.event: IND submission for the LA-PTH program
catalysts.sentence: We currently expect to submit an IND application to the FDA in late 2026.

catalysts.event: Initiation of Phase 1 SAD and MAD study for OXM injection by OPKO
catalysts.sentence: OPKO is planning to initiate a single ascending dose (SAD) and multiple ascending dose (MAD) Phase 1 clinical study with the subcutaneous injection formulation of OXM, with data expected by the end of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Osteoporosis causes over two million fractures annually in the United States.
tamClaims.sentence: Osteoporosis is a major and growing public health issue, responsible for over two million fractures annually in the United States.

tamClaims.claim: Total medical cost of osteoporotic fractures projected to increase from $57 billion in 2018 to $95 billion by 2040.
tamClaims.sentence: The total medical cost of osteoporotic fractures is projected to increase from $57 billion in 2018 to $95 billion by 2040, largely due to the aging population.

tamClaims.claim: Hypoparathyroidism affects approximately 200,000 people across the United States, the European Union and Japan.
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated that hypoparathyroidism affects approximately 200,000 people across the United States, the European Union and Japan, with approximately 43% of cases characterized as mild, 39% characterized as moderate, and 18% characterized as severe.

tamClaims.claim: Short bowel syndrome affects approximately 30,000 patients across the United States and EU.
tamClaims.sentence: Approximately 30,000 patients across the United States and EU are living with SBS.
